name: VICTOR
description: International phase 2 randomised controlled trial comparing venetoclax-low dose cytarabine with standard intensive chemotherapy (daunorubicin, cytarabine and gemtuzumab ozogamicin) in newly diagnosed AML. Adaptive design allowing staged expansion to younger patients. CRUK/19/013.
weblinkText: A trial of venetoclax with low dose cytarabine for acute myeloid leukaemia (VICTOR)
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥60 years (adaptive expansion to ≥50 years subject to interim analysis)
Diagnosis of CD33 positive AML
Genotype NPM1 mutated, FLT3-ITD negative
Fit for intensive chemotherapy with anthracyclines per treating physician
ECOG performance status 0–2
Serum creatinine ≤1.5× ULN
ALT and AST ≤2.5× ULN, bilirubin ≤2× ULN
Able to provide written informed consent
exclusionCriteria: Previous chemotherapy for AML or antecedent haematological condition (hydroxycarbamide excepted)
Other active malignancy requiring treatment
Newly diagnosed or uncontrolled HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C
Pregnancy or lactation
Females of childbearing potential (and partners) not willing to use adequate contraception during and up to 7 months after treatment
Unable to swallow tablets whole
Known hypersensitivity to investigational medicinal products
Requirement for live vaccines during treatment period
sites: 41 UK sites + Denmark, New Zealand (UK lead: University of Birmingham). Contact VICTOR@trials.bham.ac.uk for site list

name: OPTIMISE-FLT3
description: International phase 3 randomised controlled trial comparing intensive treatment strategies for newly diagnosed FLT3-mutated AML. ISRCTN34016918.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥16
Fit for intensive therapy
FLT3-ITD or FLT3-TKD mutated
New presentations requiring emergency treatment can be enrolled prior to FLT3 result
exclusionCriteria: Receipt of any previous therapy for AML or any antecedent haematological condition (the use of oral hydroxycarbamide to control white blood cell count is permitted)
Other active malignancy requiring treatment
Blast transformation of chronic myeloid leukaemia (CML)
sites: ~80 UK centres + international sites (led by Cardiff University). Contact optimise-flt3@cardiff.ac.uk for site list

iccMatch: AML with myelodysplasia related gene mutation
AML with myelodysplasia related cytogenetic abnormality

name: EVOLVE-1
description: International phase 3 randomised trial of newly diagnosed patients ineligible for intensive chemotherapy with IDH1m AML using ivosidenib plus azacitidine ± venetoclax.
inclusionCriteria: Patients with newly diagnosed IDH1-mutated AML or MDS/AML according to the 2022 International Consensus Classification
Age ≥ 18 years, no upper age limit
Ineligible for intensive induction chemotherapy by meeting at least 1 of the following criteria:
≥ 75 years of age ineligible for intensive chemotherapy per physician’s discretion (ECOG PS 0-2)
18-74 years: not eligible for standard chemotherapy with any of the following co-morbidities:
ECOG PS 2 or 3
Cardiac history of chronic heart failure requiring treatment; or with an ejection fraction ≤50%; or chronic stable angina
DLCO ≤ 65% or FEV1 ≤ 65%
Creatinine clearance ≥ 30 mL/min to <45 ml/min calculated by the Cockcroft Gault formula
Moderate hepatic impairment with total bilirubin > 1.5 to < 3.0 x upper limit of normal (ULN)
exclusionCriteria: Previous AML treatment except hydroxyurea to control WBC counts. ESA or luspatercept for MDS is allowed. Prior hypomethylating agent for MDS-EB is not allowed.
AML with BCR-ABL1; or myeloid blast crisis of CML
sites: Birmingham, Blackpool, Bristol, Cardiff, Glasgow, Leeds, Leicester, King's College Hospital London, Royal Marsden London, UCLH London, The Christie Manchester, Manchester Royal Infirmary, Freeman Hospital Newcastle, Nottingham, Churchill Hospital Oxford, Portsmouth, Southampton, Wolverhampton

name: EVOLVE-2
description: International phase 3 randomised trial of newly diagnosed patients ineligible for intensive chemotherapy with either NPM1m or KMT2Ar AML 1:1 to venetoclax plus-azacitidine ± revumenib (menin inhibitor).
inclusionCriteria: Patients with newly diagnosed NPM1-mutated or KMT2A-rearranged AML or MDS/AML according to the 2022 International Consensus Classification
Age ≥ 18 years, no upper age limit
Ineligible for intensive induction chemotherapy by meeting at least 1 of the following criteria:
≥ 75 years of age ineligible for intensive chemotherapy per physician’s discretion (ECOG PS 0-2)
18-74 years: not eligible for standard chemotherapy with any of the following co-morbidities:
ECOG PS 2 or 3
Cardiac history of chronic heart failure requiring treatment; or with an ejection fraction ≤50%; or chronic stable angina
DLCO ≤ 65% or FEV1 ≤ 65%
Creatinine clearance ≥ 30 mL/min to <45 ml/min calculated by the Cockcroft Gault formula
Moderate hepatic impairment with total bilirubin > 1.5 to < 3.0 x upper limit of normal (ULN)
exclusionCriteria: Previous AML treatment except hydroxyurea to control WBC counts. ESA or luspatercept for MDS is allowed. Prior hypomethylating agent for MDS-EB is not allowed.
AML with BCR-ABL1; or myeloid blast crisis of CML
sites: Belfast, Birmingham, Blackpool, Bristol, Cardiff, Glasgow, Leeds, Leicester, Liverpool, King's College Hospital London, St Bartholomew's Hospital London, The Christie Manchester, Newcastle, Nottingham, Churchill Hospital Oxford, Southampton, The Royal Marsden Sutton, Wolverhampton

name: AZTOUND
description: Multi-phase study of ASTX030 (oral azacitidine + cedazuridine) in combination with venetoclax in newly diagnosed AML patients ineligible for intensive chemotherapy. NCT04256317.
inclusionCriteria: Histological confirmation of newly diagnosed AML by WHO 2022 criteria
Projected life expectancy of at least 12 weeks
Ineligible for intensive induction chemotherapy: aged ≥75 years, OR aged 18–74 with ≥1 comorbidity:
Severe cardiac disorder (e.g. CHF requiring treatment, ejection fraction ≤50%, chronic stable angina)
Severe pulmonary disorder (DLCO ≤65% or FEV1 ≤65%)
Creatinine clearance ≥30 mL/min to <45 mL/min
Moderate hepatic impairment (total bilirubin >1.5 to ≤3.0× ULN)
ECOG PS 2 or 3
ECOG PS 0–2 for participants ≥75 years; ECOG PS 0–3 for 18–74 years
Able to swallow required number of tablets within 10 minutes and tolerate 4 hours fasting
exclusionCriteria: Active uncontrolled gastric or duodenal ulcer
Poor medical risk because of other conditions
Known HIV infection
Known hepatitis B or C positivity
Life-threatening illness unrelated to AML
sites: King's College Hospital London, The Christie Manchester, University Hospital Southampton

name: KOMEN-017
description: Phase 3 randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled studies assessing ziftomenib in combination with either Venetoclax+Azacitidine or intensive (7+3) therapy in patients with untreated NPM1 mutated or KMT2A-rearranged AML
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18 years
Diagnosis of AML per the 2022 WHO Classification (5th Edition)
ECOG performance status 0, 1, or 2
Adequate liver and kidney function per protocol
Non-intensive arm (Ven+Aza): Documented NPM1 mutation; ineligible for intensive therapy (≥75 years, or <75 with ECOG 2 or cardiac/renal/hepatic impairment)

Intensive arm (7+3): Documented NPM1 mutation or KMT2A rearrangement (KMT2A-PTD not eligible); FLT3 wild-type or ITD ratio <0.05 or ineligible for FLT3-targeted therapy; ejection fraction ≥50%; fit for intensive therapy
exclusionCriteria: Prior therapy for AML (except hydroxyurea or leukapheresis for WBC control)
Diagnosis of acute promyelocytic leukemia (APL), blast phase CML, or isolated myeloid sarcoma
Known BCR-ABL mutation
Active concurrent malignancies (exceptions: basal/squamous cell skin cancer, previously treated localised cancer, adjuvant hormonal therapy for prostate/breast cancer)
Active CNS involvement by AML
Clinical signs of leukostasis or WBC >25×10⁹/L prior to start of ziftomenib/placebo
Known uncontrolled HIV, active hepatitis B/C, or other uncontrolled infection
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including cardiac illness per protocol
Women who are pregnant or lactating
sites: Barts, Guys & St Thomas', Leeds, St George's, Wolverhampton

name: CAMELOT-2
description: Phase 3 randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled study of bleximenib, venetoclax and azacitidine for untreated NPM1 mutated or KMT2A-rearranged AML ineligible for intensive chemotherapy
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18 years
Previously untreated KMT2A-rearranged or NPM1-mutated AML with ≥10% bone marrow blasts per 2022 ICC criteria

Ineligible for intensive chemotherapy: ≥75 years and ineligible per physician’s discretion (ECOG PS 0-2), OR ≥18 to <75 years with ≥1 comorbidity (ECOG PS 2, severe cardiac disorder, severe pulmonary disorder, renal impairment, moderate hepatic impairment, or other comorbidity per investigator)
Adequate hepatic and renal function
Ineligibility for intensive chemotherapy should be explicitly approved by a multidisciplinary team where this is standard of care
exclusionCriteria: Diagnosis of acute promyelocytic leukemia (APL)
Known active leukemic involvement of the CNS
Recipient of solid organ transplant
Cardiac disorders: heart attack, uncontrolled/unstable chest pain, congestive heart failure, uncontrolled arrhythmia, stroke or TIA within 6 months of randomization
Active infectious hepatitis
Live attenuated vaccine within 4 weeks of randomization
Known allergies or intolerance to bleximenib, azacitidine, or venetoclax excipients
sites: Brighton, Cambridge, Canterbury, Colchester, Edinburgh, Liverpool, Guys & St Thomas', Plymouth, Worthing

name: QUANTUM-WILD
description: Phase 3, Double-Blind, Randomised, Placebo-Controlled Trial Of Quizartinib or Placebo Plus Chemotherapy in Newly Diagnosed Patients With FLT3-ITD Negative AML
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥ 18 years and ≤70
Newly diagnosed, morphologically documented primary AML based on World Health Organization (WHO) 2016 classification
ECOG performance status 0-2
Patient fit for standard “7+3” induction chemotherapy regimen
exclusionCriteria: Acute promyelocytic leukemia (APL), or BCR-ABL positive leukaemia
AML secondary to prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy
AML with known antecedent MDS, MPN or MDS/MPNs including chronic myelomonocytic leukemia (CMML), atypical chronic myeloid leukemia (aCML), juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia (JMML) and others
Newly diagnosed AML with FLT3-ITD mutations ≥5% VAF (or ≥0.05 SR) based on a validated FLT3 mutation assay
sites: Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham, King's College Hospital, Hammersmith Hospital, Maidstone, Manchester Royal Infirmary, Oxford, Torquay

iccMatch: AML with in-frame bZIP mutated CEBPA

name: CAMELOT-1
description: Phase 1/2 study of the menin inhibitor bleximenib in relapsed/refractory (R/R) NPM1 mutated or KMT2A rearranged AML
inclusionCriteria: Phase 1: Age ≥2 years (paediatric cohort), ≥18 years (all other cohorts)
Phase 2: Age ≥18 years
Relapsed or refractory acute leukemia harboring KMT2A, NPM1 or NUP98/NUP214 alterations (Phase 1)
Phase 2: R/R AML per WHO 2022 with KMT2A rearrangement or NPM1 mutation only
ECOG performance status 0, 1, or 2
WBC ≤20×10⁹/L (Phase 1) or adequate per protocol (Phase 2)
eGFR ≥30 mL/min (adults), >50 mL/min (paediatric)
Adequate contraception required per protocol
exclusionCriteria: Acute promyelocytic leukemia, Down syndrome-associated leukemia, or juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia
Active CNS disease
Prior solid organ transplantation
QTcF ≥450 ms (males) or ≥470 ms (females); family history of Long QT syndrome
Allogeneic stem cell transplant ≤3 months before first dose, or evidence of GVHD, or requiring immunosuppressant therapy post-HSCT
Donor lymphocyte infusion ≤28 days before first dose
Prior cancer immunotherapy within 4 weeks (blinatumomab within 2 weeks)
sites: Cardiff, The Christie, Liverpool, Guys & St Thomas', Leeds, Oxford, Plymouth, UCLH

name: DSP-5336
description: Phase 1/2 study of the menin inhibitor enzomenib in relapsed/refractory (R/R) AML, ALL, MDS, and MM with KMT2A, NPM1, or other qualifying alterations
inclusionCriteria: Phase 1: R/R AML, ALL, acute leukemia of ambiguous lineage (WHO 2022), or MDS/MM at selected sites; documented KMT2A fusion or NPM1 mutation (in regions where required)
Phase 2: R/R AML or ALL with ≥5% bone marrow blasts and documented KMT2A fusion; must not have had prior menin inhibitor exposure
Age ≥18 years (or ≥12 years at selected sites for monotherapy)
ECOG performance status ≤2
WBC <30,000/µL (monotherapy) or <25,000/µL (combinations)
Creatinine clearance ≥50 mL/min (CKD-EPI formula)
Total bilirubin ≤1.5× ULN (≤2.0× for Gilbert’s syndrome)
AST and ALT ≤3.0× ULN
Estimated life expectancy ≥3 months
exclusionCriteria: Left ventricular ejection fraction <50%
Acute promyelocytic leukemia
Systemic calcineurin inhibitors within 2 weeks prior to first dose
QTc >480 ms (or ≥470 ms females / ≥450 ms males at UK sites); history of prolonged QT syndrome
Active uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infection requiring parenteral therapy; must be afebrile with negative blood cultures ≥72 hours prior to Cycle 1 Day 1
Concurrent sensitive substrates or strong inhibitors/inducers of CYP3A4/5 (ketoconazole, isavuconazole, itraconazole excluded; other standard antifungals permitted)
Major surgery within 28 days prior to first dose
Active CNS leukemia (prophylactic intrathecal chemotherapy allowed)
HSCT or CAR-T therapy within 60 days (UK sites: CAR-T within 6 months); active GVHD or post-HSCT immunosuppression
Known detectable viral load for HIV or hepatitis C, or evidence of active hepatitis B
Severe dysphagia, short-gut syndrome, gastroparesis, or conditions limiting oral drug absorption
sites: Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham, Bristol, Edinburgh, King's College Hospital, Sarah Cannon Research Institute London, UCLH, The Christie Manchester, Oxford, Stoke-on-Trent, The Royal Marsden Sutton

name: APL0498
description: Phase 1 study to investigate the safety and efficacy of APL-4098 alone, and in combination with azacitidine or azacitidine and venetoclax in AML and MDS/AML
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18

R/R AML (primary or secondary, including treatment-related), participant is intolerant to, or considered ineligible for available therapies known to provide clinical benefit. Must have ≥5% blasts in BM or ≥1% blasts in the peripheral blood.
MDS/AML defined as diagnosis of MDS with 10-19% BM blasts who have not had a response after at least four cycles of treatment with a monotherapy hypomethylating agent (HMA)
MDS with 5%-9% bone marrow blasts who are not benefiting with monotherapy hypomethylating agents (Phase 1 only; MDS-EB patients will not be enrolled in the Phase 2 part of the study)
exclusionCriteria: Allogeneic transplant within 6 months of screening
sites: The Royal Marsden London, Sarah Cannon Research Institute London

name: JNJ-89853413
description: Phase 1/2 study of a CD33xVδ2 T-Cell Engager for Relapsed or Refractory Acute Myeloid Leukemia or Myelodysplastic Neoplasms
inclusionCriteria: Diagnosis per WHO 2022: R/R AML or R/R moderate-high, high, or very-high risk MDS per IPSS-M
Age ≥18 years
Body weight ≥40 kg
ECOG performance status 0-2
eGFR ≥40 mL/min (CKD-EPI)
Laboratory parameters within required range per protocol
exclusionCriteria: Clinically significant pulmonary compromise or current supplemental oxygen use
Uncontrolled systemic viral, bacterial, or fungal infection
Known allergies or intolerance to JNJ-89853413 excipients
Major surgery or significant traumatic injury within 14 days of first dose
Active CNS involvement
sites: Addenbrooke's Hospital Cambridge, UCLH London, The Christie Manchester

name: MOMENTUM
description: Phase 1/2 study of AZD3632 monotherapy or in combination with anticancer agents in participants with advanced haematologic malignancies with KMT2Ar, NPM1m, or other genotypes associated with HOX overexpression
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥16 years
Adequate organ function
Module 1 (dose escalation): Diagnosis of acute leukemia or MDS harbouring genetic alterations associated with upregulation of HOX; backfill requires KMT2A rearrangement or NPM1 mutation
R/R after standard of care therapy including ≥2 cycles of intensive chemotherapy, HMA monotherapy, or HMA/venetoclax combinations
R/R MDS: ≥5% bone marrow blasts and/or persistence of peripheral blasts after ≥2 cycles of HMA
WBC <25,000/µL (cytoreduction permitted per protocol)
ECOG performance status ≤2
Life expectancy ≥8 weeks
exclusionCriteria: Burkitt lymphoma/leukaemia or acute promyelocytic leukemia
Active testicular or active CNS involvement (>CNS1 or radiographic) by leukaemia
Unresolved treatment-related toxicities Grade ≥2 from prior therapy
Abnormal levels of potassium or magnesium prior to first dose
Module 1: Non-CNS radiation within 2 weeks, CNS radiation within 8 weeks of first dose; prior menin inhibitor exposure (backfill only)
Module 2: Posaconazole contraindicated per local prescribing information
sites: Edinburgh, London, The Christie Manchester, Newcastle

name: BioDriveAFS
description: BioDrive seeks to identify the most effective way to prevent and detect fungal infection in patients with acute leukaemia (acute myeloid leukaemia, acute lymphoblastic leukaemia, high-risk myelodysplastic syndromes and transformed myeloproliferative neoplasms); assessing if monitoring the risk of fungal infection by regular blood tests is safe and reduces the need to use antifungal drugs.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥16 years

Diagnosis of new, or relapsed, acute leukaemia or haematological disorder judged to need chemotherapy by the patient’s clinical care team, including AML, ALL, HRMDS, or AML transformation of a myeloproliferative neoplasm (tMPN)

Patient expected to have prolonged neutropenia related to chemotherapy which would mandate either antifungal prophylaxis and/or systematic invasive fungal infection biomarker monitoring (at least weekly)
exclusionCriteria: Previous proven or probable invasive fungal infection (IFI) (according to the EORTC/MSG criteria)
Contraindication to all potential prophylactic antifungal agents
Planned chemotherapy using any regimen that mandates the use of systemic antifungal medication
sites: ~40 UK NHS haematology departments (ISRCTN11633399). Contact biodrive-group@york.ac.uk for site list

name: PROPEL
description: PROPEL is a multicentre randomised controlled trial comparing best practice usual care (BPUC) with a personalised prehabilitation care package (PPCP) incorporating a 12-month internal pilot, parallel process evaluation and economic evaluation. NIHR134257. Chief Investigators: Prof Janet Dunn, Prof Simon Stanworth.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥ 16 years, treated on adult AML pathway
Diagnosis of either AML or MDS-EB2 (MDS with ≥10% blasts in the bone marrow) following first diagnosis or disease relapse
In complete morphological remission (defined <5% blasts in bone marrow)
Planned to receive either: Intensive treatment (e.g. anthracycline/cytarabine based chemotherapy and/or HSCT), or lower intensity treatment (i.e. Venetoclax based treatment) with HSCT – Pathway 1
Or: Planned to receive lower intensity treatment without HSCT (e.g. Venetoclax or Ivosidenib based treatment) - Pathway 2
Prior to, or within the 1st course of treatment following remission
Planned to receive at least one further full cycle of treatment (chemotherapy or HSCT) post randomisation
Access to the internet and an email address
Willing to use videoconferencing to undertake the appointments and sessions
exclusionCriteria: Diagnosis of Acute Promyelocytic Leukaemia
Undergoing single agent Azacitidine, single agent low dose Cytarabine, Menin inhibitors or FLT3 inhibitor treatment without planned HSCT
sites: Multiple UK centres (NIHR134257). Contact propel@warwick.ac.uk for site list

name: MO-TRANS
description: MO-TRANS is a randomised multi-centre phase 3 trial of mocravimod maintenance treatment post allogeneic stem cell transplant
inclusionCriteria: AML according to WHO 2022 classification
ELN high-risk or intermediate-risk in CR1, any risk AML in CR2
Planned allogeneic transplant from 9/10-10/10 HLA-matched donor or haploidentical donor with use of PBSC graft
Any conditioning regime with a Transplant conditioning Score (TCI) ≥ 1.5
Use of Tacrolimus-based GVHD prophylaxis
Age ≥18 and ≤75
exclusionCriteria: Use of ciclosporin, anti-thymocyte globulin (ATG), alemtuzumab or abatacept for GVHD prophylaxis
Macular oedema
sites: Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham, University Hospital of Wales Cardiff, Queen Elizabeth University Hospital Glasgow, University College Hospital London, Imperial College London, Manchester Royal Infirmary, Freeman Hospital Newcastle, Nottingham University Hospitals, Oxford University Hospitals, Royal Hallamshire Hospital Sheffield

name: GRACE
description: Phase 1/2 study assessing granulocyte augmentation to single-cord blood transplantation for high risk AML
inclusionCriteria: Fit, transplant-eligible patients, aged <55
High risk AML or MDS/AML (as defined by ICC 2022) fitting one or more of the following criteria:
TP53 mutation (single- or multi-hit)
Presence of inv(3)(q21.3q26.2) or t(3;3)(q21.3;q26.2)
Adverse risk (as per ICC 2022) and >0.1% MRD by flow cytometry after 2 cycles of induction
AML (any risk) with partial remission (<10% blasts) after 2 cycles induction
Early relapse (<6 months) after chemotherapy alone (excluding t(16;16), inv(16) or t(8;21))
Bone marrow performed within 28 days of starting conditioning chemotherapy demonstrates either: <10% blasts; or >10% blasts with a hypocellular background
FEV1 >65% without dyspnoea on mild activity
AST/ALT <3 x ULN, Bilirubin <1.5 x ULN (excluding Gilbert’s syndrome)
Performance Status (ECOG) of 0 or 1
exclusionCriteria: AML secondary to a myeloproliferative neoplasm
Active CNS disease
Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant
sites: King's College Hospital London, The Royal Marsden London, The Christie Manchester

name: Inobrodib (CCS1477)
description: Phase 1/2a adaptive open-label study of CCS1477 (inobrodib), a first-in-class p300/CBP bromodomain inhibitor, as monotherapy and in combination with azacitidine ± venetoclax in relapsed/refractory AML and higher-risk MDS. NCT04068597. CRUK 17062.
weblinkText: A trial of inobrodib for some types of blood cancer
inclusionCriteria: Confirmed relapsed or refractory haematological malignancy (AML or higher-risk MDS)
Prior standard therapy received
ECOG 0-2
Adequate organ function
exclusionCriteria: Chemotherapy/investigational agents within 14 days or 5 half-lives
Major surgery within 4 weeks
Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors within 2 weeks or inducers within 4 weeks
Statins must be discontinued
Unresolved toxicities >CTCAE Grade 1 (except alopecia, Grade 2 neuropathy)
Severe/uncontrolled systemic diseases
QTcF >470 msec
sites: 18 UK sites (recruiting): Royal Marsden Sutton, Clatterbridge Cancer Centre, University Hospitals Bristol, University Hospital of Wales Cardiff, Royal Derby Hospital, Western General Hospital Edinburgh, Gartnavel General Glasgow, Leicester Royal Infirmary, St Bartholomew's London, Sarah Cannon Research Institute London, NIHR UCL London, Imperial College London, Christie Manchester, Newcastle upon Tyne Hospitals, Nottingham University Hospitals, Cancer and Haematology Centre Oxford, University Hospital Southampton, Royal Stoke University Hospital

name: EP0042
description: Phase 1/2a modular study of EP0042, a dual FLT3 and Aurora kinase inhibitor, as monotherapy and in combination with venetoclax ± azacitidine in relapsed/refractory FLT3-wildtype AML, CMML, and MDS. NCT04581512.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18 with histological/cytological AML confirmation
FLT3 wildtype, relapsed/refractory AML
Mutation status known at trial entry
Failed all approved therapies or ineligible/intolerant
ECOG 0-2
Life expectancy sufficient to complete ≥1 cycle (28 days)
CrCl ≥50 mL/min
Adequate liver function
exclusionCriteria: APL (FAB M3)
Symptomatic/untreated brain/leptomeningeal metastases
Systemic anti-cancer therapy within 2 weeks
Allo/auto transplantation within 90 days or active immunosuppression for GVHD within 2 weeks
CrCl <50 mL/min
Inadequate liver function: bilirubin ≥1.5× ULN, or ALT/AST ≥3× ULN
sites: 3 UK sites (recruiting): University College London Hospital, The Royal Marsden London, The Christie Manchester. Plus sites in Australia and Netherlands.

name: AUGMENT-101 (Revumenib)
description: Phase 1/2 open-label dose escalation and expansion study of revumenib (SNDX-5613), a menin inhibitor, in relapsed/refractory acute leukemias harbouring KMT2A rearrangements, NPM1 mutations, or NUP98 rearrangements. 447 patients across 57+ sites. Led to FDA approval of revumenib. NCT04065399.
inclusionCriteria: Active acute leukemia (bone marrow blasts ≥5% or blasts in peripheral blood)
Documented R/R disease per NCCN guidelines (Phase 2)
KMT2A rearrangement, NPM1 mutation, or NUP98 rearrangement/HOX upregulation
WBC <25,000/µL at enrollment
Age ≥30 days (Cohort 2D: ≥18 yrs, body weight ≥40 kg)
ECOG 0-2 or Karnofsky/Lansky ≥50
exclusionCriteria: Active APL
Active CNS disease
HIV, HBV or HCV
Cardiac disease within 6 months: MI, unstable angina, CHF (NYHA ≥II), QTc >450 ms
GI issues affecting oral absorption; cirrhosis Child-Pugh B or C
GVHD >Grade 0 within 4 weeks
Pregnant or nursing
sites: 57+ sites across US, Australia, Canada, France, Germany, Israel, Italy, Lithuania, Netherlands, Spain. No UK sites currently.

name: KOMET-007 (Ziftomenib)
description: Phase 1 study of ziftomenib (menin inhibitor, FDA-approved for R/R NPM1m AML) in combination with venetoclax/azacitidine, venetoclax alone, or 7+3 chemotherapy ± quizartinib in newly diagnosed and R/R AML with NPM1 mutations or KMT2A rearrangements. 420 patients. NCT05735184.
inclusionCriteria: Documented NPM1 mutation or KMT2A rearrangement
Newly diagnosed or R/R AML
ECOG 0-2
Adequate liver, renal, and cardiac function
Arm C: NPM1-mutated and FLT3-ITD+ with allelic ratio ≥0.05
exclusionCriteria: APL or blast phase CML; known BCR-ABL
Active CNS involvement by AML
Leukostasis or WBC >25,000/µL (cytoreduction permitted)
Active HIV, HBV, or HCV
QTcF >480 ms (Arms A/B) or >450 ms (Arm C)
Active GVHD requiring >0.5 mg/kg prednisone
Pregnant or lactating
sites: 45+ US sites only. No UK sites currently.

name: PyramIDH
description: Phase 3, open-label, randomised, non-comparative study of ivosidenib monotherapy versus azacitidine monotherapy in HMA-naive adults with IDH1-mutated MDS. Prior Phase 2 showed 72% overall response rate for ivosidenib. 48 patients. NCT06465953.
inclusionCriteria: Diagnosis of HMA-naive IDH1 R132-mutated MDS per WHO 5th edition
Moderate-high, high, or very-high risk per IPSS-M (any blast count 0-19%)
For Low/Moderate-Low risk: cytopenias (platelets <100, ANC <1000, Hb <10), blasts 5-19%, eligible for HMA
Age ≥18
exclusionCriteria: Prior anticancer/disease-modifying treatment for MDS (HMAs, chemo, HSCT, IDH1 inhibitors)
>20% blasts
For LR-MDS: prior growth factors, luspatercept, lenalidomide, imetelstat are allowed
sites: 6 UK sites: University College London Hospital (recruiting), King's College Hospital London (recruiting), Churchill Hospital Oxford (recruiting), Western General Hospital Edinburgh (not yet recruiting), Torbay Hospital (not yet recruiting).

name: RENEW (Elritercept)
description: Phase 3, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled study of elritercept (KER-050/TAK-226) for transfusion-dependent anaemia in very low-, low-, or intermediate-risk MDS. 225 patients globally. NCT06499285.
inclusionCriteria: MDS per WHO 2016, IPSS-R very low/low/intermediate
Transfusion dependent: LTB (4-7 RBC units/16 weeks) or HTB (≥8 units/16 weeks)
Refractory/intolerant to prior ESA, or unlikely to respond (EPO >200 U/L)
<5% bone marrow blasts
ECOG 0-2
exclusionCriteria: Del(5q) or therapy-related MDS
Prior luspatercept, sotatercept, or elritercept
Prior HMAs, IDH inhibitors, lenalidomide, imetelstat, or immunosuppressive therapy for MDS
Platelet >450 or ≤25 ×10⁹/L; ANC ≤500
NYHA Class III/IV; QTcF >500ms; BMI ≥40
History of AML; EF <35%
sites: 8 UK sites (all recruiting): Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham, Addenbrooke's Hospital Cambridge, St James's University Hospital Leeds, King's College Hospital London, Sarah Cannon Research Institute London, Christie Manchester, Nottingham City Hospital, Churchill Hospital Oxford.

name: MITHRIDATE
description: Phase 3, randomised, open-label, multicentre international trial comparing ruxolitinib with either hydroxycarbamide or interferon-alpha as first-line therapy for high-risk polycythaemia vera (PV). 586 patients. NCT04116502.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18, PV per WHO criteria within past 15 years
JAK2 mutation positive
High-risk PV: WBC >11 ×10⁹/L AND at least one of: age >60, prior thrombosis/haemorrhage, platelets >1000, hypertension or diabetes requiring pharmacotherapy
Hb >8 g/dL
May have had ONE cytoreductive therapy <10 years (if not resistant/intolerant)
exclusionCriteria: PV diagnosis >15 years ago
Absence of JAK2 mutation
>1 cytoreductive therapy or duration >10 years or resistance/intolerance
Active infection (HIV, HBV, HCV, TB)
ECOG ≥3
Transformation to myelofibrosis
Prior ruxolitinib
Platelets <100 or neutrophils <1 in last 12 months (not therapy-related)
sites: 47 UK sites (recruiting): Aberdeen, Bath, Belfast, Birmingham Heartlands, Blackpool, Bournemouth, Bristol Southmead, Cambridge Addenbrooke's, Canterbury, Cardiff, Chichester, Colchester, Cottingham Castle Hill, Dudley Russells Hall, Edinburgh Western General, Exeter, Gloucester, Inverness Raigmore, Kettering, Leicester, Livingston, London (UCH, Guy's, St George's), Manchester Wythenshawe, Wirral, Middlesbrough James Cook, Newcastle Freeman, Newport, North Tyneside, Northampton, Norwich, Nottingham, Oxford Churchill, Reading, Runcorn, Slough, Southampton, Stoke, Sunderland, Sutton Coldfield Good Hope, Truro, Warwick, Wolverhampton, Worthing.

name: VERIFY (Rusfertide)
description: Phase 3 study of rusfertide (PTG-300), a hepcidin mimetic, versus placebo as add-on to existing PV therapy for haematocrit control in phlebotomy-dependent polycythaemia vera. 293 patients. NCT05210790. Active, not currently recruiting.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18, PV per WHO 2016 criteria
≥3 phlebotomies in 6 months OR ≥5 in 1 year before randomisation
Stable PV therapy regimen if on cytoreductive therapy
exclusionCriteria: Phlebotomy required at Hct <45%
Clinically significant thrombosis within 2 months
Active/chronic bleeding within 2 months
Invasive malignancy within 5 years
Prior busulfan, pipobroman, or ³²-phosphorus within 7 months
sites: 8 UK sites: Hillingdon Hospital Uxbridge, Barnet Hospital, Pilgrim Hospital Boston, Northwick Park Hospital Harrow, Royal London Hospital, University College London Hospital, Royal Free Hospital London, Churchill Hospital Oxford.

name: GIV-IN-PV (Givinostat)
description: Phase 3, randomised, open-label study comparing givinostat (HDAC inhibitor) versus hydroxyurea in JAK2V617F-positive high-risk polycythaemia vera patients. 220 patients over 48-week core phase. NCT06093672.
inclusionCriteria: PV per WHO 2016 criteria, JAK2V617F-positive
High-risk (age ≥60 and/or prior thrombosis) within 3 years of screening
Treatment need: Hct ≥45% (or <45% with phlebotomy in prior 3 months), WBC >10, or platelets >400
Normalised haematocrit (<45%) at randomisation
exclusionCriteria: Prior HU with documented resistance/intolerance per ELN criteria
QTcF >450ms (males) or >460ms (females)
Splanchnic/cerebral venous sinus thrombosis or prior splenectomy
Prior JAK2 inhibitor, HDAC inhibitor, or ³²-phosphorus therapy
MI, stroke, unstable angina within 6 months
Uncontrolled hypertriglyceridaemia (TG >1.5× ULN)
sites: 5 UK sites (recruiting): Pilgrim Hospital Boston, Imperial College Healthcare London, Milton Keynes University Hospital, Southampton General Hospital, University of Oxford/WIMM.

name: IMPRSSION (Sapablursen)
description: Phase 2a, randomised, open-label study of sapablursen (TMPRSS6 antisense inhibitor) for reducing phlebotomy frequency and improving quality of life in phlebotomy-dependent polycythaemia vera. 50 patients. NCT05143957. Active, not currently recruiting.
inclusionCriteria: Meet modified WHO 2016 diagnostic criteria for PV
Must be phlebotomy dependent
Cytoreductive therapy: discontinued ≥3 months pre-screening with resolved AEs, OR stable dose for ≥3 months (hydroxyurea, interferon, ruxolitinib)
exclusionCriteria: Meets criteria for post-polycythaemia vera myelofibrosis (PPV-MF)
Moderate to severe splenic pain or spleen-related organ obstruction
Active or chronic bleeding within 1 month
Known primary or secondary immunodeficiency
Active HIV, hepatitis C, or hepatitis B
Malignancy within 5 years
Surgery requiring general anaesthesia within 1 month
sites: 2 UK sites: Oxford University Hospitals, University College London Hospitals.

name: Bomedemstat (SHORESPAN-006)
description: Phase 3, randomised, open-label study of bomedemstat (LSD1 inhibitor) versus best available therapy in essential thrombocythaemia with inadequate response to or intolerance of hydroxyurea. 340 patients. NCT06079879.
inclusionCriteria: Diagnosis of ET per WHO 2016 diagnostic criteria (confirmed by central pathologist)
Centrally assessed bone marrow fibrosis score Grade 0 or Grade 1
History of inadequate response to or intolerance of hydroxyurea (modified ELN criteria)
Platelet count >450 ×10⁹/L
Up to 3 prior ET-directed cytoreductive agents including hydroxyurea
Age ≥18
exclusionCriteria: Known hypersensitivity to bomedemstat or LSDi/MAOi
History of GI function impairment interfering with drug absorption
Evidence of increased risk of bleeding at screening
History of malignancy unless curative treatment completed with no evidence for 2 years
HIV-infected with history of Kaposi's sarcoma/Multicentric Castleman's Disease
sites: 10 UK sites: Pilgrim Hospital Boston (recruiting), Addenbrooke's Cambridge, Gloucestershire Royal Hospital, Lincoln County Hospital, University College London Hospital, Guy's & St Thomas' London, King's College Hospital London, Royal Gwent Hospital Newport, GenesisCare Windsor, Christie Manchester.

name: STP938 (Dencatistat)
description: Phase 1b, open-label study of STP938 (CTPS1 inhibitor) in adults with high-risk essential thrombocythaemia who cannot tolerate or have failed hydroxycarbamide. 20 patients. NCT06786234.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18
Confirmed diagnosis of ET per WHO or BSH criteria
High-risk ET
Raised platelet count (>600 ×10⁹/L) requiring cytoreductive therapy
Neutrophil count >1.0 ×10⁹/L
Either intolerant of or resistant to hydroxycarbamide
Adequate hepatic function and blood coagulation parameters
ECOG 0-2
exclusionCriteria: Currently or recently receiving cytoreductive therapy for ET (wash out periods apply)
Investigational agents for ET within 4 weeks
Proven or suspected transformation to PV, myelofibrosis, MDS, AML or another myeloid neoplasm
Known malabsorption syndrome
Previous splenectomy
Any uncontrolled intercurrent illness
Active hepatitis B or HCV infection
QTcF >470 msec
sites: 6 UK sites: University Hospital of Wales Cardiff (recruiting), Imperial College/Hammersmith Hospital London (recruiting), Sarah Cannon Research Institute London (recruiting), Churchill Hospital Oxford (recruiting), University of Southampton Hospital (recruiting), Royal Hallamshire Hospital Sheffield (not yet recruiting). Plus 7 sites in France.

name: PACIFICA (Pacritinib)
description: Phase 3, randomised, controlled study of pacritinib versus physician's choice in myelofibrosis with severe thrombocytopenia (platelets <50,000/µL). 399 patients. NCT03165734.
inclusionCriteria: PMF, post-PV MF, or post-ET MF
Platelets <50,000/µL
DIPSS Int-1, Int-2, or High-risk
Palpable spleen ≥5cm below costal margin
TSS ≥10 (MPN-SAF TSS 2.0) or single symptom ≥5 or two symptoms ≥3
ECOG 0-2, peripheral blasts <10%, ANC ≥500
LVEF ≥50%
exclusionCriteria: Life expectancy <6 months
Prior pacritinib; >1 prior JAK2 inhibitor
Prior ruxolitinib at >10mg daily for >90 days within 120 days of Day 1
History of splenectomy; QTcF >450ms; NYHA Class II-IV
Active GI/bowel disorders interfering with absorption
sites: 7 UK sites: Barts Health/Royal London Hospital London (recruiting), Guy's and St Thomas' Hospital London (recruiting), Hammersmith Hospital/Imperial College London (recruiting), Churchill Hospital Oxford (recruiting), Gloucestershire Royal Hospital (recruiting). Royal Hallamshire Sheffield and Beatson Glasgow completed.

name: IMpactMF (Imetelstat)
description: Phase 3, randomised, open-label study of imetelstat (telomerase inhibitor) versus best available therapy in intermediate-2 or high-risk myelofibrosis relapsed/refractory to JAK inhibitor. 327 patients. NCT04576156. Active, not currently recruiting.
inclusionCriteria: PMF, post-ET/PV MF per WHO or IWG-MRT criteria
DIPSS Int-2 or High-risk
Relapsed/refractory to JAK inhibitor (≥6 months at optimal dose or ≥3 months at maximal doses)
Measurable splenomegaly: palpable ≥5cm OR volume ≥450cm³
Active symptoms on MFSAF v4.0 (score ≥5)
ECOG 0-2
exclusionCriteria: Peripheral or bone marrow blasts ≥10%
Prior imetelstat
MF-directed therapy or JAK inhibitor within 14 days
Other malignancy unless disease-free ≥3 years
Active hepatitis or HIV
Major surgery within 28 days
sites: 3 UK sites: St Bartholomew's Hospital London, Guy's and St Thomas' Hospital London, Oxford University Hospitals.

name: POIESIS (Navtemadlin)
description: Phase 3, randomised, double-blind, add-on study of navtemadlin (MDM2 inhibitor) plus ruxolitinib versus placebo plus ruxolitinib in JAK inhibitor-naive myelofibrosis patients with suboptimal response to ruxolitinib. Requires TP53 wild-type. 600 patients. NCT06479135.
inclusionCriteria: Confirmed PMF, post-PV MF, or post-ET MF per WHO criteria
High, Intermediate-1, or Intermediate-2 risk (IPSS)
ECOG 0-2
JAK inhibitor treatment naive
TP53 wild-type as assessed by central testing (for randomisation)
Suboptimal response to ruxolitinib run-in
exclusionCriteria: Prior splenectomy
Splenic irradiation within 3 months
Prior BCL-XL, BET, MDM2, PI3K, PIM, or XPO1 inhibitor therapy or p53-directed therapy
Eligible for bone marrow transplant
Peripheral blood or bone marrow blast count ≥10%
TP53 mutation (excluded from randomisation)
sites: 14 UK sites: Birmingham Heartlands, Lincoln County Hospital, Pilgrim Hospital Boston, Addenbrooke's Cambridge, University Hospital of Wales Cardiff, Western General Hospital Edinburgh, Gloucestershire Royal Hospital, Harrogate District Hospital, Leicester Royal Infirmary, Clatterbridge Cancer Centre Liverpool, University College Hospital London, Guy's Hospital London, GenesisCare Oxford, GenesisCare Windsor.

name: JNJ-88549968 (CALR Bispecific)
description: Phase 1, first-in-human study of JNJ-88549968, a T-cell redirecting bispecific antibody targeting CD3 and mutant calreticulin (CALR) on MPN cancer cells, in CALR-mutated essential thrombocythaemia and myelofibrosis. 220 patients. NCT06150157.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18
Positive for a CALR driver mutation of ET or MF
ET: failed ≥2 lines including hydroxyurea
MF: failed ≥1 JAK inhibitor
ECOG ≤2
exclusionCriteria: Known allergies/hypersensitivity to excipients
Concurrent or recently diagnosed malignancies (standard exceptions)
Prior solid organ transplantation
Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant ≤6 months or evidence of GVHD requiring immunosuppression
History of clinically significant cardiovascular disease within 6 months
sites: 3 UK sites (all recruiting): University College London Hospitals, Guy's and St Thomas' Hospital London, Churchill Hospital Oxford. Plus sites in US, Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Israel.

name: CARDINAL (TERN-701)
description: Phase 1/2 study of TERN-701, a highly selective allosteric BCR-ABL1 inhibitor, in previously treated chronic-phase CML. Includes mutation cohort targeting T315I. 75% MMR rate reported at RP2D. 180 patients. NCT06163430.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18
ECOG 0-2
Cytopathologically confirmed BCR-ABL1 positive CML in chronic phase
At least one prior TKI with treatment failure, suboptimal response, or intolerance
Prior asciminib allowed
Adequate organ function
exclusionCriteria: Systemic antineoplastic therapy or experimental therapy within 7 days
Unresolved clinically significant toxicity from prior therapy (>Grade 2 or baseline)
sites: 1 UK site: Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust, London (recruiting). Plus sites in US, Australia, France, Germany, Italy, New Zealand, South Korea, Spain.

name: PAXIS (Pacritinib for VEXAS)
description: Phase 2, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled dose-finding study of pacritinib (IRAK1/JAK2 inhibitor) in VEXAS syndrome. 24-week double-blind period followed by 48-week open-label period. Requires UBA1 mutation. 78 patients. NCT06782373.
inclusionCriteria: Documented pathogenic UBA1 mutation at M41 or neighbouring splice site (c.118-1, c.118-2)
Current or documented past (within 6 months) inflammatory involvement
Ongoing glucocorticoid therapy: stable prednisone/prednisolone 15-45 mg/day
Karnofsky Performance Status ≥50%
AST/ALT ≤3× ULN; bilirubin ≤4× ULN; CrCl ≥30 mL/min
ANC ≥500/µL; platelets ≥25 ×10⁹/L; peripheral blasts <5%
exclusionCriteria: Prior allo-HSCT or solid organ transplant
>1 ICU admission for VEXAS flare in prior 6 months
≥9 RBC transfusion units in 90 days pre-enrollment
Known concurrent high/very high-risk MDS (IPSS-R) requiring antineoplastic treatment
HMA exposure within 6 months OR >6 lifetime cycles
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease
Arterial/venous thrombotic events within 60 days
sites: 4 UK sites: St James's University Hospital Leeds (recruiting), King's College Hospital London (recruiting), Royal Free Hospital London (not yet recruiting), Churchill Hospital Oxford (not yet recruiting). Plus sites in US, Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, Spain.

name: IMPACT-AML
description: Phase 3, multicentre, randomised, open-label, pragmatic trial comparing high-intensity reinduction chemotherapy with low-intensity therapies (VenAza, Ven+LDAC, gilteritinib, etc.) in 1st or 2nd relapse AML. EU-funded. 339 patients. NCT06713837.
inclusionCriteria: Non-APL AML per WHO 2022 or ICC 2022
1st or 2nd relapse or refractory per ELN 2022
Clinically candidate for both low-intensity and high-dose chemotherapy
Both treatment arms available per local practice
Age >18
ECOG <4
exclusionCriteria: Known contraindication to selected study drug
Participation in another clinical trial with investigational agents within 14 days or 5 drug half-lives
Active infections or other clinical conditions making patient ineligible per investigator
sites: 22 sites in Italy, 6 in Germany, 5 in Czechia, plus Lithuania, Portugal, Romania, Spain. No UK sites currently.

name: FEDORA
description: Phase 2, open-label study of fedratinib (JAK2 inhibitor) combined with ropeginterferon alfa-2b in treatment-naive, JAK2 V617F-mutated myelofibrosis. Met its primary tolerability endpoint. 30 patients across 13 UK TAP centres. ISRCTN88102629.
inclusionCriteria: Treatment-naive myelofibrosis
JAK2 V617F mutation positive
Details to be confirmed from full protocol
exclusionCriteria: Details to be confirmed from full protocol
sites: 13 UK TAP centres including UCL. Contact via ISRCTN registry for site list.

name: AMMO (ASTX727)
description: Phase 2, randomised UK trial of oral decitabine/cedazuridine (ASTX727) versus hydroxycarbamide/best supportive care in CMML and MDS/MPN overlap syndromes. Showed superior ORR (53% vs 30%) and PFS (23.5 vs 13.9 months). 13 UK hospitals.
inclusionCriteria: CMML or MDS/MPN overlap syndrome
Details to be confirmed from full protocol
exclusionCriteria: Details to be confirmed from full protocol
sites: 13 UK hospitals: Christie Manchester, Churchill Hospital Oxford, Belfast City Hospital, St James's Leeds, QE Birmingham, Aberdeen Royal Infirmary, King's College Hospital London, Nottingham City Hospital, Southampton General, UCLH London, University Hospital of Wales Cardiff, Beatson Glasgow, Sheffield Teaching Hospitals.
